title: prompt-less signing via passphrase file
body:
To support signing images without prompting the user, add CLI flags for
providing a passphrase file.

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

comments.body: I should have read deeper into my inbox, per @vrothberg this will address #1261

@mtrmac updated:
* using github.com/proglottis/gpgme
* the env variable is now pointing to a passphrase file instead

I am cool using an env var. This way, all consumers support it by simply vendoring c/image.

> I am cool using an env var. This way, all consumers support it by simply vendoring c/image.

I am unhappy with c/image providing options that are _only_ configurable using environment variables. (To be fair, there are a few such variables like HOME, but at least those are clearly not expected to differ within a process; that’s not the case with the passphrase.)

We don’t do that for every single option in `SystemContext`. What makes this one different? Most of the precedents like `--authfile` at least _allow_ using a CLI option.
@mtrmac PTanotherL, now with CLI options.  Once we're cool with the code, I will take care of updating the docs as well.  Tests have been extended accordingly.
Could we add a `--gpg-passphrase-file` or `--gpg-file` option too?  I'm not married to either name.  That might be handy in some circumstances.  Would that allay your concerns @mtrmac ?

> Could we add a `--gpg-passphrase-file` or `--gpg-file` option too? I'm not married to either name. That might be handy in some circumstances. Would that allay your concerns @mtrmac ?

That's what the PR already does. There's a `--sign-passphrase-file` for copy/syn and a `--passphrase-file` for standalone-sign

* vendored c/image/v5@main
* updated tests and added one with a wrong passphrase-file
* updated docs
* addressed previous comments on phrasing

> Please update completions/bash/skopeo as well.

Sigh ... I still need to do that.
A few man page nits to pick before merging.
